mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
The Google Cloud SDK provides the necessary tools to interact with GKE and manage your Google Cloud resources.

```bash

curl -sSL https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

gcloud init

```

> This command installs the Google Cloud SDK.
> Reload the shell to access the gcloud command.
> Initialize the gcloud environment and authenticate.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count in a given region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure the cluster name matches the one created.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the GKE cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step involves setting up autoscaling and monitoring for your GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=us-central1

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-cloud-logging --enable-cloud-monitoring --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust min and max nodes as needed.
> Enable logging and monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your data, cordon the nodes, upgrade the cluster version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> This upgrades the cluster to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not running or kubeconfig is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the user account does not have the necessary permissions to perform the action.

**Fix:**
Ensure your account has the required IAM roles assigned in Google Cloud.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the resources have not been created or are in a different namespace.

**Fix:**
Check the namespace with 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the cluster is still initializing or if there are issues with the nodes.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the nodes with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure they are in 'Ready' state.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for GKE Autopilot mode, improved workload identity features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new Autopilot features and adjust configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and authenticated.
